motivation: Understanding base constructs for package orchestration & system modules.
whyExists: Websites must work on devices from 320px (small phones) to 4K monitors (3840px). Users access sites from phones 60%+ of the time. Google penalizes non-mobile-friendly sites in search rankings. CSS layout systems make it possible to build one codebase that works everywhere. Before Flexbox and Grid, layouts used float hacks and table-based designs — fragile and hard to maintain.
realWorldAnalogy:
Imagine you're arranging furniture in a room. You want chairs in a row (like Flexbox — one direction). Or you want a grid of rooms like an apartment building (like CSS Grid — rows AND columns).

Flexbox: "Put these chairs in a line, space them evenly, center them."
CSS Grid: "Make a 3-column, 4-row layout for this page."
simpleExplanation: To learn CSS Core Principles Part 10A from first principles, start by looking at how data resolves at runtime. Imagine your processor executing instructions sequentially. CSS Core Principles Part 10A defines how values bind, operations evaluate, and stack structures are clean.
visualDiagram:
```text
[User Input] ──► [Process Scope: CSS Core Principles Part 10A] ──► [Memory Address] ──► [Console Output]
```
syntaxExplanation: Verify that all brackets, colons, keywords, and assignments match the css syntax specifications. Avoid naming collisions by using scoped namespaces.

stepByStepExecution.desc: Allocate memory segment and load bindings.
